message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The estimates of future course of spreading of the SARS-CoV-2 virus are frequently based on Markovian models in which the transitions between the compartments are exponentially distributed. Specifically, the basic reproduction number
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    is also determined from formulae where it is related to the parameters of such models. The observations show that the start of infectivity of an individual appears nearly at the same time as the onset of symptoms, while the distribution of the incubation period is not an exponential.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    We propose a method for estimation of
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    for COVID-19 based on the empirical incubation period distribution and assumed very short infectivity period that lasts only few days around the onset of symptoms. It is tested on daily new cases in six major countries in Europe, in the first wave of epidemic in spring, 2020.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The calculations show that even if the infectivity starts two days before the onset of symptoms and stops immediately when they appear, the value of
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    is larger than that from the classical, Markovian approach. For more realistic cases, when only individuals with mild symptoms spread the virus for few days after onset of symptoms, the respective values are even larger.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The calculations of
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    and other characteristics of spreading of COVID-19 based on the classical, Markovian approaches should be taken very cautiously. Instead, non-Markovian models with general distribution functions of transition between compartments should be considered as more appropriate.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Key messages</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        Although formulae for estimate of the basic reproduction number
                        <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                        <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                        , by using general-form functions of infectivity are known since the earliest works in epidemiology, majority of studies are based on exponential distribution function.
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        We introduce a new methodology of calculating
                        <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                        <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                        with an infectivity function obtained by combining empirical incubation period distribution with infectivity window function that is localized around the onset of symptoms.
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        Estimates of
                        <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                        <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                        for the first wave of COVID - 19 in the spring 2020, by the proposed methodology are larger than those from the classical SIR model.
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        When possible, the estimates of
                        <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                        <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                        should be based on empirical distributions of the infectivity functions, while the values obtained with the conventional epidemic spreading models should be taken with caution.
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: Estimation of the basic reproduction number of COVID-19 from the incubation period distribution
